description: Poison Ivy is a remote access tool, first released in 2005 but unchanged since 2008. It includes features common to most Windows-based RATs, including key logging, screen capturing, video capturing, file transfers, system administration, password theft, and traffic relaying.
name: Poison Ivy
summary:
The object contains information about a type of malware known as Poison Ivy, which is classified as a remote access tool. It was first released in 2005 but has remained unchanged since 2008. The malware includes features common to most Windows-based RATs, such as key logging, screen capturing, video capturing, file transfers, system administration, password theft, and traffic relaying.

The specific details of Poison Ivy's capabilities include remote access functionality, allowing an attacker to gain control over a compromised device remotely. It also has the ability to capture and record sensitive information, including keystrokes, screen content, and network activity.

In terms of its classification, Poison Ivy is identified as being from a specific family of malware. The object does not contain any additional details about this family or their related threats.

description: Calamine is a set of free tools to help organizations detect and examine Poison Ivy infections on their systems. The package includes these components: PIVY callback-decoding tool (ChopShop Module) and PIVY memory-decoding tool (PIVY PyCommand Script).
external_references: [{"source_name": "Calamine ChopShop Module", "description": "The FireEye Poison Ivy decoder checks the beginning of each TCP session for possible PIVY challengeresponse sequences. If found, the module will try to validate the response using one or more passwords supplied as arguments.", "url": "https://github.com/fireeye/chopshop"}, {"source_name": "Calamine PyCommand Script", "description": "Helps locate the PIVY password.", "url": "https://github.com/fireeye/pycommands"}]
name: Analyze with FireEye Calamine Toolset
summary:
The object contains information about a set of free tools to help organizations detect and examine Poison Ivy infections on their systems. This package includes two components: the PIVY callback-decoding tool, also known as the ChopShop Module, and the PIVY memory-decoding tool, which is represented by the PyCommand Script.

The FireEye Poison Ivy decoder in the ChopShop Module checks the beginning of each TCP session for possible PIVY challenge-response sequences. If such sequences are found, the module will try to validate them using one or more passwords supplied as arguments. On the other hand, the PyCommand Script helps locate the PIVY password.

These tools were originally developed by FireEye and have been made available through a GitHub repository. The Calamine toolset is designed to assist organizations in detecting and examining Poison Ivy infections on their systems.
